title: CNN: Newly Released Surveillance Video Further Undermines ICE's Account of Minneapolis Shooting
excerpt: CNN and the New York Times published surveillance footage of the January 14 Minneapolis shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis showing a confrontation lasting roughly 12 seconds — contradicting the agents' sworn account of a three-minute struggle — with the allegedly used snow shovel visible on the ground for the duration of the encounter.
content:
In early April 2026, newly obtained surveillance footage of the January 14 Minneapolis shooting was published by CNN and the New York Times, providing the clearest visual evidence yet that sworn ICE testimony about the encounter was false.

  • The nine-minute video showed the struggle between the federal agent and the two Venezuelan men lasted approximately 12 seconds — directly contradicting the ICE officer's sworn claim of a three-minute struggle during which he was beaten with a snow shovel and broom.
  • The shovel, visible in the footage, was on the snowy ground for the entirety of the encounter. Federal prosecutors had access to this footage within hours of the shooting but reviewed it only weeks after filing criminal charges against the two men.
  • ICE confirmed that the two officers who made sworn statements about the incident had been placed on administrative leave and remained under investigation by the U.S. Attorney's Office for potential criminal prosecution.

The publication of the footage reinforced concerns about a broader pattern of false or unsupported official narratives in ICE use-of-force cases, and added pressure on federal investigators to accelerate the perjury probe.
